overview.description: AI-powered satellite imagery analysis platform for defense, intelligence, and construction monitoring
overview.description_long: SpaceKnow empowers decision-makers with ultra large-scale planetary analysis through its proprietary AI-powered analytics engine, combined with the world's most comprehensive collection of earth observation imagery. Their SpaceKnow Guardian platform integrates multi-source satellite data with proprietary AI algorithms to deliver automated monitoring, early warning systems, and actionable intelligence. Key use cases include defense & intelligence surveillance, construction progress monitoring, maritime tracking, and economic indicator indices (e.g., Satellite Manufacturing Index cited by Bloomberg, Barron's, and WSJ). SpaceKnow is incorporated in New York with an R&D branch office in Prague.

history.origin_story: Founded in 2015 to index the physical world through satellite imagery and AI-powered geospatial analytics. Incorporated in New York with core R&D team based in Prague, Czech Republic.

key_milestones.event: Founded in New York with R&D branch in Prague
Featured in TechCrunch for self-serve geospatial analytics platform; CEO Pavel Machalek introduces multi-provider satellite imagery approach
Satellite data on China factory activity cited by Bloomberg during COVID-19 pandemic
ESA GEMSTONE project partnership to expand commercial nowcasting capabilities
RACE dashboard published in partnership with ESA for EU recovery monitoring
Russia-Ukraine border monitoring published attracting global media attention
Dubai flooding analysis via satellite imagery published
SpaceKnow Guardian platform launched for construction monitoring and defense intelligence

risk_assessment.agi_displacement_rationale: SpaceKnow's core value is in proprietary multi-source satellite data aggregation, specialized CV algorithms, and a decade of annotated imagery for specific object detection (planes, ships, vehicles, construction). Foundation models can increasingly analyze images, but SpaceKnow's moat lies in real-time satellite tasking pipelines, specialized training data, and domain expertise in defense/intelligence workflows — areas foundation models alone cannot easily replicate.
risk_assessment.market_risk_rationale: Geospatial intelligence is a proven, growing market with strong demand from government/defense, financial analytics, and infrastructure monitoring. SpaceKnow has established media credibility (Bloomberg, Barron's, WSJ, Wired) and ESA partnerships that validate real-world demand and product-market fit.
risk_assessment.funding_risk_rationale: No disclosed external funding rounds found — the company appears to be bootstrapped or has undisclosed angel/seed investment. The company has been operating since 2015 and continues to hire, suggesting sustainable revenue, but lack of disclosed funding creates uncertainty about runway and scaling capacity.
risk_assessment.overall_risk_rationale: SpaceKnow has a defensible position in a real and growing market with strong media validation and ESA partnerships, but the small team size (~34 employees), undisclosed funding, and increasing commoditization of satellite image analysis by large cloud providers (AWS, Google, Microsoft) creates meaningful medium-term risk.

metadata.notes: Company is incorporated in New York (SpaceKnow Inc., 450 Lexington Ave, 4th floor, NY 10017) with an R&D branch in Prague (Thamova 289/13, Prague 18600). LinkedIn reports 51-200 employee range but shows 34 linked employees; using 34/11-50 range as more accurate. Founded year 2015 per LinkedIn (company website says '13 years of expertise' which would suggest ~2013 from 2026). No funding rounds found in public sources.
